channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Prosecutors Fail to Bring Charges Against Democrats Involved in Illegal Orders Video
item.description: The rejection was a remarkable rebuke, suggesting that ordinary citizens did not believe that the lawmakers had committed any crimes.

item.title: Portland Lawsuit Alleges Tear Gas Use by ICE Is a Health Threat
item.description: A novel lawsuit in Portland argues the chemicals are a health threat that have soaked into apartment walls, furniture and even children’s toys.

item.title: Easing Unemployment Supports Fed Extending Pause on Rate Cuts
item.description: January’s jobs data, released on Wednesday, bolstered expectations that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady for longer than previously...

item.title: BlackRock offers DeFi trading for the first time, buys Uniswap tokens
item.description: The tie-up, which also relies on the crypto firm Securitize, is the first of its kind between Wall Street and Defi.

item.title: Job Growth Was Overstated, New Data Shows
item.description: Annual revisions show that employers added far fewer jobs in 2024 and 2025 than previously estimated.

item.title: The 4 step approach Google’s AI boss used to return the tech giant to a ‘golden era’ of innovation
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: ‘The fiscal trajectory is not sustainable’: CBO warns about the highest debt in U.S. history as Trump adds $1.4 trillion to 10-year deficit
item.description: The national debt is set to surpass the historical high of 106% of GDP that it reached in 1946 by 2030, according to the CBO.

item.title: Canada Mass Shooting: What to Know About the Home and School Shootings in Tumbler Ridge, B.C.
item.description: The attack at a school and a residence in the small, remote town of Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, has shocked a country where such acts of violence...

item.title: Nine People Killed in Mass Shooting in British Columbia, Canada
item.description: Nine people were killed and 25 others were injured in the third-deadliest shooting in Canada’s history. The police said the person believed to be the ...

item.title: 9 Killed and 25 Injured in Shootings in Tumbler Ridge, Canada
item.description: The police said the suspect died of a self-inflicted injury after the shooting in Tumbler Ridge, a remote community in British Columbia.

item.title: Students and Teachers Hid in School for Hours During British Columbia Shooting
item.description: The shooting in Tumbler Ridge was one of the deadliest in Canada’s history. Seven people, including the suspected shooter, were found dead at the loca...

item.title: Iran Commemorates Revolution, With U.S. Warships Lurking Off the Coast
item.description: The authoritarian clerical regime in Tehran came to power in 1979. Today, it presides over a country that is deeply polarized and under threat of an A...

item.title: What Questions Do You Have About Saudi Arabia?
item.description: “Ask a Correspondent” will take your questions to Vivian Nereim, our Gulf bureau chief.

item.title: Crypto crime-fighting startup TRM Labs notches $1 billion valuation with new $70 million funding round
item.description: With its blockchain analytics tools, TRM Labs helps private companies and agencies like the FBI track illicit finance.

item.title: Bitcoin demand in Nancy Guthrie disappearance shows how crypto is becoming a more frequent feature of physical crimes
item.description: TMZ and an Arizona news outlet reported receiving the ransom letter.
